reforms.description: The German government adopted measures to stimulate research and development, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, with DM 4.2 billion earmarked for this purpose over the next four years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases funding for R&D in SMEs, which can enhance innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government adopted measures to stimulate research and development.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in R&D, targeting small and medium-sized enterprises, which are crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In early 1978 the Government also adopted measures to stimulate research and development, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises; furthermore, a Bill was submitted to Parliament providing for special incentives for energy saving modifications in existing buildings.
